question_text: A 3 kg object is thrown upward with a velocity that gives it 120 J of kinetic energy. Calculate the velocity of the object. (Use the formula KE = 0.5 × mass × velocity²)
explanation: Rearranging KE = 0.5 × m × v², we get v = √(2 × KE / m). So v = √(2 × 120 / 3) = √80 = 8.94 m/s. This shows how kinetic energy depends on both mass and velocity.
